summary:
Yes, Kell alloimmunization in pregnancy is strongly associated with fetal thrombocytopenia due to maternally derived anti-Kell IgG antibodies crossing the placenta and causing neonatal alloimmune thrombocytopenia (NAIT).

Okay, I'm going to summarize Agent1’s reasoning as requested:

Kell alloimmunization in pregnancy leads to production of maternal anti-Kell IgG antibodies which cross the placental barrier and cause fetal thrombocytopenia through neonatal alloimmune thrombocytopenia (NAIT), making it one of the most significant causes of this condition. This association has been well-documented across multiple medical references confirming its strong link between immune responses targeting Kell antigens and adverse hematologic outcomes for fetuses.
